description: Studies that explore the books written before the coming of Christ — from the Law and the Prophets to the Writings. The Old Testament tells the story of God's covenant with Israel and points forward to the Messiah.

sections.description: The first five books of the Bible (the Pentateuch), covering creation, the fall, the exodus, and the giving of the Law — the foundation on which the rest of Scripture builds.

subsections.name: Genesis
subsections.description: The book of beginnings: creation, the fall, and the patriarchs — Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph.

subsections.name: Exodus
subsections.description: God rescues his people from slavery in Egypt, reveals his name, and gives his Law at Sinai.

subsections.name: Leviticus
subsections.description: Instructions for worship and holiness, showing how a sinful people can draw near to a holy God.

subsections.name: Numbers
subsections.description: Israel's wilderness journey — its failures, its discipline, and God's continued faithfulness.

subsections.name: Deuteronomy
subsections.description: Moses' farewell sermons, renewing the covenant and preparing Israel to enter the Promised Land.

sections.description: The historical books trace Israel's story from the conquest of Canaan through the monarchy, exile, and return — showing God's faithfulness and his people's repeated unfaithfulness.

subsections.name: Joshua
subsections.description: The conquest and division of the Promised Land under Joshua's leadership.

subsections.name: Judges
subsections.description: A cycle of rebellion, oppression, and rescue as Israel repeatedly turns from God.

subsections.name: Ruth
subsections.description: A story of loyalty, providence, and redemption set in the time of the judges.

subsections.name: 1 Samuel
subsections.description: The transition from judges to kings: Samuel, Saul, and the rise of David.

subsections.name: 2 Samuel
subsections.description: David's reign — his triumphs, failures, and God's covenant with his house.

subsections.name: 1 Kings
subsections.description: Solomon's glory and the kingdom's division; Elijah and the contest on Mount Carmel.

subsections.name: 2 Kings
subsections.description: The decline and fall of both kingdoms, ending in exile.

subsections.name: 1 Chronicles
subsections.description: A retelling of Israel's history from Adam to David, focused on worship and the temple.

subsections.name: 2 Chronicles
subsections.description: Solomon and the temple, the divided kingdom, and the hope of restoration.

subsections.name: Ezra
subsections.description: The return from exile and the rebuilding of the temple under Ezra's leadership.

subsections.name: Nehemiah
subsections.description: The rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls and the renewal of the covenant community.

subsections.name: Esther
subsections.description: God's hidden providence protecting his people in the Persian court.

sections.description: Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Song of Songs — books that explore the depths of human experience: suffering, praise, wisdom, and love.

subsections.name: Job
subsections.description: A profound exploration of suffering, justice, and the mystery of God's ways.

subsections.name: Psalms
subsections.description: Israel's songbook and prayer book — the full range of human emotion brought before God.

subsections.name: Proverbs
subsections.description: Practical wisdom for everyday life, rooted in the fear of the Lord.

subsections.name: Ecclesiastes
subsections.description: A searching examination of meaning, work, and mortality 'under the sun'.

subsections.name: Song of Songs
subsections.description: A celebration of love and marriage, rich with imagery of delight and longing.

sections.description: The prophetic books call Israel to repentance, warn of judgement, and point forward to God's ultimate redemption. They are essential for understanding the New Testament's fulfilment of those promises.

subsections.name: Isaiah
subsections.description: Judgement and breathtaking hope — including the Servant Songs that point to Christ.

subsections.name: Jeremiah
subsections.description: A prophet's anguish as Jerusalem falls, and the promise of a new covenant.

subsections.name: Lamentations
subsections.description: Raw grief over the destruction of Jerusalem, held together by hope in God's faithfulness.

subsections.name: Ezekiel
subsections.description: Visions of God's glory departing and returning, and the promise of spiritual renewal.

subsections.name: Daniel
subsections.description: Faithful presence in exile, and visions of God's sovereign kingdom over all nations.

subsections.name: Hosea
subsections.description: God's unfailing love for an unfaithful people, pictured through Hosea's own marriage.

subsections.name: Joel
subsections.description: A locust plague as a call to repentance, and the promise of the Spirit.

subsections.name: Amos
subsections.description: A sharp prophetic call for justice and the warning that empty religion will not stand.

subsections.name: Obadiah
subsections.description: A brief oracle against Edom and the coming day of the Lord.

subsections.name: Jonah
subsections.description: God's compassion for the nations — and a reluctant prophet's resistance to it.

subsections.name: Micah
subsections.description: Justice, mercy, and humility — and the promise of a ruler from Bethlehem.

subsections.name: Nahum
subsections.description: The fall of Nineveh and the comfort of God's justice for his oppressed people.

subsections.name: Habakkuk
subsections.description: Wrestling honestly with God over injustice, and learning to trust in his timing.

subsections.name: Zephaniah
subsections.description: The day of the Lord and the joy of restoration for a purified remnant.

subsections.name: Haggai
subsections.description: A call to rebuild the temple and prioritise God's house over personal comfort.

subsections.name: Zechariah
subsections.description: Visions of restoration and a Messiah who comes humbly on a donkey.

subsections.name: Malachi
subsections.description: A final call to faithfulness before the long silence before John the Baptist.

description: Studies in the books written after the coming of Christ — the Gospels, the letters of the apostles, and Revelation. The New Testament reveals the fulfilment of God's promises in Jesus and the life of the early church.

sections.description: The four accounts of Jesus's life, death, and resurrection, and the story of the early church's expansion across the Roman world.

subsections.name: Matthew
subsections.description: Jesus as the fulfilment of the Old Testament, the new Moses, and the King of God's kingdom.

subsections.name: Mark
subsections.description: A fast-paced account of Jesus as the suffering Servant and Son of God.

subsections.name: Luke
subsections.description: Jesus as Saviour of the world — with special attention to the poor, women, and outsiders.

subsections.name: John
subsections.description: The deepest theological portrait of Jesus — the Word made flesh, the I AM, the Son of God.

subsections.name: Acts
subsections.description: The Holy Spirit empowers the church to take the gospel from Jerusalem to the ends of the earth.

sections.description: Paul's letters to churches and individuals — exploring the logic of the gospel, Christian conduct, and the nature of the church.

subsections.name: Romans
subsections.description: Paul's most systematic exposition of the gospel: sin, justification, sanctification, and hope.

subsections.name: 1 Corinthians
subsections.description: Addressing divisions and disorders in Corinth, with teaching on love, gifts, and the resurrection.

subsections.name: 2 Corinthians
subsections.description: Paul's defence of his ministry and the paradox of strength through weakness.

subsections.name: Galatians
subsections.description: A passionate defence of justification by faith alone against those adding the law.

subsections.name: Ephesians
subsections.description: The riches of the believer's identity in Christ, and the church as God's new humanity.

subsections.name: Philippians
subsections.description: Joy in all circumstances and the example of Christ's humility.

subsections.name: Colossians
subsections.description: Christ as supreme over all things, and the implications for everyday life.

subsections.name: 1 Thessalonians
subsections.description: Encouragement for a young church, with teaching on the return of Christ.

subsections.name: 2 Thessalonians
subsections.description: Correction of misunderstandings about the end times, and a call to faithful work.

subsections.name: 1 Timothy
subsections.description: Instructions for church life, leadership, and sound doctrine.

subsections.name: 2 Timothy
subsections.description: Paul's final letter — a charge to Timothy to guard the gospel and finish well.

subsections.name: Titus
subsections.description: Appointing leaders and teaching that sound doctrine leads to godly living.

subsections.name: Philemon
subsections.description: A personal appeal for reconciliation and the social implications of the gospel.

sections.description: Letters from James, Peter, John, Jude, and others — covering faith in trials, genuine belief, false teaching, and the ultimate hope of Revelation.

subsections.name: Hebrews
subsections.description: Jesus as the fulfilment of all the Old Testament's priesthood, sacrifice, and covenant — superior in every way.

subsections.name: James
subsections.description: Faith that works — practical wisdom for living as God's people in the world.

subsections.name: 1 Peter
subsections.description: Hope and holy living for exiles in a hostile world.

subsections.name: 2 Peter
subsections.description: Warnings against false teachers and confidence in God's promises.

subsections.name: 1 John
subsections.description: Assurance of salvation and the marks of genuine faith: love, obedience, and truth.

subsections.name: 2 John
subsections.description: A brief letter on love, truth, and not welcoming those who deny Christ.

subsections.name: 3 John
subsections.description: Commending faithful hospitality and warning against self-seeking leadership.

subsections.name: Jude
subsections.description: A strong call to contend for the faith against false teaching that has crept into the church.

subsections.name: Revelation
subsections.description: Visions of God's ultimate victory over evil, the judgement of the wicked, and the renewal of all things.

description: Studies focused on Christian doctrine — what the Bible teaches about God, humanity, salvation, and the future. Grounding ourselves in right belief is the foundation for right living.

sections.description: Introductory studies surveying the whole of Christian teaching — the gospel, the core doctrines, and the historic confessions of the church.

subsections.name: Foundations
subsections.description: The essential building blocks of Christian belief — what every believer needs to know.

subsections.name: The Gospel
subsections.description: Studies focused on the heart of the Christian message: sin, grace, and salvation through Christ.

subsections.name: Creeds & Confessions
subsections.description: The historic statements of faith that have defined orthodox Christianity across the centuries.

sections.description: Who is God? Studies exploring the Trinity, God's attributes, and what it means to know him personally.

subsections.name: Attributes of God
subsections.description: God's holiness, love, sovereignty, justice, and the other characteristics Scripture reveals.

subsections.name: The Trinity
subsections.description: One God in three persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — and why this matters.

subsections.name: God the Father
subsections.description: God as Creator, King, and Father — and what it means to be his child.

sections.description: Studies on the Bible itself — how it came to be, what it claims about itself, and how to read it well.

subsections.name: Authority
subsections.description: Why the Bible has the right to speak into every area of life.

subsections.name: Inspiration
subsections.description: How God worked through human authors to give us his Word.

subsections.name: Canon
subsections.description: How the books of the Bible were recognised and gathered together.

subsections.name: Interpretation
subsections.description: Principles for reading the Bible faithfully and accurately.

sections.description: Studies on what it means to be human — made in God's image, embodied, and created for a purpose.

subsections.name: Creation
subsections.description: God as Creator of all things — and what that means for how we view the world.

subsections.name: Image of God
subsections.description: What it means to be made in God's likeness, and the dignity that gives every person.

subsections.name: Embodiment
subsections.description: The goodness of having a body, and what Scripture says about physical existence.

subsections.name: Purpose
subsections.description: Why we are here — the human calling to love God, serve others, and steward creation.

sections.description: Studies on sin — its origin, its nature, and its devastating effects on human beings and the world.

subsections.name: Nature of Sin
subsections.description: What sin actually is — not just rule-breaking, but a fundamental turning away from God.

subsections.name: The Fall
subsections.description: What happened in Genesis 3, and how it affects every human being.

subsections.name: Temptation
subsections.description: How temptation works, and how believers can stand firm against it.

sections.description: Studies on the person of Jesus Christ — fully God and fully human — and what his coming means.

subsections.name: Person of Christ
subsections.description: The doctrine of the two natures — how Jesus is both fully divine and fully human.

subsections.name: Incarnation
subsections.description: Why the Son of God became flesh, and what his humanity means for us.

sections.description: How does God rescue sinners? Studies covering atonement, justification, adoption, and the whole sweep of what God does to save us.

subsections.name: Atonement
subsections.description: How Christ's death deals with sin — substitution, redemption, reconciliation, and more.

subsections.name: Conversion
subsections.description: What it means to turn from sin and trust in Christ — repentance and faith.

subsections.name: Justification
subsections.description: Being declared righteous before God — by grace alone, through faith alone, in Christ alone.

subsections.name: Adoption
subsections.description: The extraordinary gift of being brought into God's family as his sons and daughters.

sections.description: Studies on the Holy Spirit — who he is, what he does, and how he works in believers and the church.

subsections.name: Person of the Spirit
subsections.description: The Holy Spirit as the third person of the Trinity — not a force, but a person.

subsections.name: Fruit of the Spirit
subsections.description: The character qualities the Spirit produces in those who walk with him.

subsections.name: Spiritual Gifts
subsections.description: The gifts the Spirit gives to equip believers for service in the body of Christ.

sections.description: What is the church, and why does it matter? Studies on the nature, mission, and structure of the body of Christ.

subsections.name: Nature of Church
subsections.description: What the church is — the people of God, the body of Christ, the temple of the Spirit.

subsections.name: Sacraments
subsections.description: Baptism and the Lord's Supper — what they are, what they mean, and how to observe them.

subsections.name: Leadership
subsections.description: Biblical patterns of church leadership: elders, deacons, and servant-hearted authority.

subsections.name: Worship
subsections.description: What Christian worship is, why it matters, and how it shapes the life of the community.

sections.description: Studies on death, resurrection, judgement, and the new creation — the hope that shapes how Christians live in the present.

subsections.name: Return of Christ
subsections.description: The certain hope that Jesus will return in glory to judge the living and the dead.

subsections.name: Judgment
subsections.description: God's final verdict on all people — and the good news that Christ has borne judgment for his own.

subsections.name: Heaven & Hell
subsections.description: The eternal destinies Scripture describes — and why they matter for how we live now.

description: Studies that connect biblical truth to the everyday experience of following Christ — in relationships, work, identity, and the ongoing struggle against sin.

sections.description: Practices that train us to stay close to God and grow in godliness — not ways to earn favour, but habits that keep us open to his grace.

subsections.name: Prayer
subsections.description: Talking with God — its foundations, its forms, and how to develop a richer prayer life.

subsections.name: Bible Reading
subsections.description: How to read Scripture well — with understanding, consistency, and an expectation of being changed.

subsections.name: Worship
subsections.description: Worship as a whole-life posture, not just a Sunday activity.

sections.description: The ongoing process of becoming more like Christ — fighting sin, growing in virtue, and understanding who we are in him.

subsections.name: Fruit of the Spirit
subsections.description: Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control.

subsections.name: Overcoming Sin
subsections.description: Practical and theological help for dealing with persistent patterns of sin.

subsections.name: Growth in Grace
subsections.description: What it looks like to grow as a Christian — and what gets in the way.

subsections.name: Identity in Christ
subsections.description: Who we are because of what Christ has done — and why that changes everything.

sections.description: Studies on the relationships God has placed us in — marriage, family, friendship, and community.

subsections.name: Marriage
subsections.description: The covenant of marriage — its purpose, its challenges, and its picture of Christ and the church.

subsections.name: Singleness
subsections.description: Singleness as a full and meaningful calling, not a waiting room for marriage.

subsections.name: Parenting
subsections.description: Raising children in the faith — with grace, truth, and a long-term perspective.

subsections.name: Friendship
subsections.description: The gift of Christian friendship — and how to cultivate relationships that go deep.

subsections.name: Communication
subsections.description: Speaking truthfully and kindly — and listening well.

subsections.name: Community
subsections.description: What it means to live as the body of Christ — bearing one another's burdens.

sections.description: Studies applying the gospel to work, money, time, and ambition — seeing everyday labour as part of God's purposes.

subsections.name: Work Life
subsections.description: What the Bible says about work — its goodness, its frustrations, and its redemption in Christ.

subsections.name: Money & Possessions
subsections.description: Generosity, contentment, and a biblical view of wealth.

subsections.name: Stewardship of Time
subsections.description: How we use our time — and how the gospel reshapes our priorities.

subsections.name: Ambition
subsections.description: Holy ambition vs. selfish ambition — and how to tell the difference.

subsections.name: Retirement
subsections.description: What faithful living looks like in the later seasons of life.

sections.description: How believers think and act on the pressing ethical questions of our time — from social justice to bioethics to technology.

subsections.name: Social Justice
subsections.description: God's heart for the vulnerable and the oppressed, and what that means for us.

subsections.name: Bioethics
subsections.description: Beginning and end of life issues — approached with theological care and compassion.

subsections.name: Engaging Technology
subsections.description: How to use technology faithfully in a world that is shaped by it.

sections.description: Studies that help believers walk through pain, uncertainty, and difficulty with honesty and hope.

subsections.name: Trials and Affliction
subsections.description: Why God allows suffering, and how Scripture equips us to endure it with hope.

subsections.name: Discerning God's Will
subsections.description: How to make wise decisions — and what Scripture actually promises about guidance.

subsections.name: Mental Health
subsections.description: Depression, anxiety, and other struggles — approached with compassion and biblical care.

subsections.name: Grief
subsections.description: How to grieve as those who have hope — honestly, and not alone.

description: Studies for those involved in serving others — in evangelism, pastoral care, teaching, and leadership. Rooted in Scripture, these studies equip believers to serve effectively and sustainably.

sections.description: The biblical foundations for all Christian ministry — what it means to serve, and what kind of person the servant must be.

subsections.name: Servant Leadership
subsections.description: Leading as Jesus led — with humility, sacrifice, and care for those being served.

subsections.name: Calling
subsections.description: Understanding your calling to ministry — how it is discerned and what it involves.

sections.description: Studies for those called to explain the Bible publicly — covering method, character, and the weight of handling God's Word.

subsections.name: Expository Preaching
subsections.description: How to preach faithfully from the text — letting the Bible set the agenda.

subsections.name: Leading Bible Studies
subsections.description: How to lead a group well — asking good questions and helping people meet God in the text.

sections.description: The practical and theological dimensions of leading a church or ministry — vision, teams, conflict, and governance.

subsections.name: Vision & Strategy
subsections.description: How to think clearly about where God is leading and how to get there together.

subsections.name: Team Building
subsections.description: Gathering, developing, and sustaining a healthy ministry team.

subsections.name: Conflict Resolution
subsections.description: How to handle disagreement and division in a way that honours God and preserves unity.

subsections.name: Church Governance
subsections.description: The structures and processes that help a church be accountable and well-ordered.

sections.description: Studies equipping believers to walk alongside one another in difficulty, loss, crisis, and need.

subsections.name: Pastoral Conversations
subsections.description: The skills and posture needed for helpful one-to-one ministry conversations.

subsections.name: Crisis Intervention
subsections.description: How to respond wisely and compassionately when someone is in acute distress.

subsections.name: Visitation
subsections.description: The ministry of presence — visiting the sick, the elderly, and the housebound.

subsections.name: Marriage Prep
subsections.description: Helping couples prepare for a marriage built on Christ.

subsections.name: Bereavement Care
subsections.description: Walking with those who are grieving — what to say, what not to say, and how to stay.

sections.description: Studies on taking the gospel to those who haven't heard it — personally, locally, and across cultures.

subsections.name: Personal Witnessing
subsections.description: How to share your faith naturally and clearly in everyday conversation.

subsections.name: Apologetics
subsections.description: Answering the questions that keep people from taking Christianity seriously.

subsections.name: Cross-Cultural Ministry
subsections.description: Serving and sharing across cultural boundaries with sensitivity and respect.

subsections.name: Church Planting
subsections.description: The theology and practice of establishing new gospel communities.

sections.description: Studies on the ministry of helping others grow — one to one, in small groups, and by training the next generation.

subsections.name: One-to-One Ministry
subsections.description: The power of intentional, personal discipleship — meeting regularly to open the Bible together.

subsections.name: Small Group Leading
subsections.description: How to lead a small group that is genuine, growing, and grounded in Scripture.

subsections.name: Training Others
subsections.description: Equipping others to do the work of ministry — multiplying not just converts but leaders.

description: Studies that don't fit neatly into another category — or whose shelf information hasn't been set yet.
